step_by_step_reasoning: We analyze the architecture based on the transcript and whiteboard. 1. The customer (UserCompanyDataStream) sends data. 2. Ingestion paths: directly to Splunk (via standard HEC ELB), serverless via Lambda (to HEC ELB), or via Kinesis Data Firehose (to a dedicated sticky ELB). 3. The ELBs route data to Splunk Indexers running on EC2 instances with Auto Scaling. 4. Storage tiering: EC2 instances cache data locally and evict cold data to Splunk's S3 bucket, or archive to S3 (Glacier tier). 5. Dynamic Data Self Storage: customers can export data directly from Splunk's S3 bucket to their own S3 bucket.

graph.name: Splunk Cloud SmartStore and Tiered Storage Architecture
graph.notes: Separates compute and storage to optimize performance and cost using EC2 caching, S3, and Glacier, while enabling direct data export to customer-owned S3 buckets.

nodes.name: Customer Data
nodes.notes: Source data from customer applications and services.

nodes.name: Ingestion Lambda
nodes.notes: Serverless ingestion mechanism sending data directly to Splunk.

nodes.name: Kinesis Data Firehose
nodes.notes: Streams data from AWS sources to Splunk with sticky load balancing requirements.

nodes.name: HEC Load Balancer
nodes.notes: Handles standard HTTP Event Collector (HEC) traffic and Lambda ingestion.

nodes.name: Sticky Load Balancer
nodes.notes: Handles Firehose traffic which requires sticky sessions.

nodes.name: Splunk Indexers (ASG)
nodes.notes: Compute layer with fast caching tiers for quick dashboard and search performance.

nodes.name: Splunk S3 Bucket
nodes.notes: SmartStore cold storage tier for data replication and durability.

nodes.name: Splunk Glacier Bucket
nodes.notes: Archival storage tier, allows self-service rehydration via UI.

nodes.name: Customer S3 Bucket
nodes.notes: Destination for dynamic data self-storage exports.

edges.notes: Lambda forwards data to HEC load balancer.
Firehose routes through dedicated sticky load balancer.
Routes sticky stream traffic to indexers.
Evicts cold data from EC2 cache to S3.
Archives compliance and long-term data to Glacier.
Dynamic data self-storage export to customer's account.
